title: ericameria nauseosa var. graveolens
intro: Ericameria nauseosa var. graveolens, also known as piney rubber rabbitbrush, is a shrub native to the western United States and Canada, characterized by its small yellow flowers and distinctive piney scent. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic qualities, which have been traditionally used to treat various health issues. The health benefits of Ericameria nauseosa var. graveolens include reducing inflammation, soothing skin irritations, and providing relief from respiratory issues such as colds and bronchitis. The plant can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or salve, allowing for various herbal preparations to harness its medicinal properties.
